channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: King and Queen of England Visit White House
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived at the White House, where President Trump and Melania Trump welcomed them at the start of a four-day state visit marking America’s 250th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump and the first lady, Melania Trump, welcoming King Charles III and Queen Camilla to the White House on Monday.

item.title: Con ustedes, el nuevo líder del mundo libre
item.description: Volodímir Zelenski les está mostrando a los países europeos, y a otras naciones, cómo el mundo libre pos-Estados Unidos puede preservar su libertad e independencia.
item.dc:creator: David French
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Leon Neal/Getty Images

item.title: ¿Cómo enfrentará BTS una gira mundial agotadora?
item.description: El antiguo entrenador del grupo comparó a sus integrantes con atletas profesionales. “Es un trabajo que somete al cuerpo a mucho más esfuerzo del que la mayoría de la gente cree”.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado

media:content.media:credit: Bighit Music y Netflix, vía Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: BTS ofreció un concierto de regreso en el centro de Seúl a finales de marzo, su primera aparición como grupo tras una pausa de cuatro años.

item.title: Today’s Spelling Bee Hints for April 28, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Picture Prompts: Cherry Blossoms
item.description: What are the signs of spring where you live?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Late Night Downloads the White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: The event “was supposed to be an evening of fun and merriment,” Jon Stewart said, “until, like most things in America, it was interrupted by gunfire.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Qué significa analogous
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 57 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: analogous
item.description: This word has appeared in 54 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: La madre que no habla
item.description: Cuando su hijo de 11 años desapareció el año pasado, Jacqueline Pritchett se negó incluso a reconocer su existencia.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: La desaparición de Jacob Pritchett, de 11 años, ha desconcertado a los agentes de policía, quienes se sienten frustrados porque su madre, Jacqueline Pritchett, se niega a reconocer que él alguna vez existió.

item.title: It’s Been Centuries Since Haiti’s Revolution. It’s Still Paying for It.
item.description: Haiti’s revolution shocked the world. America still isn’t over it.
item.dc:creator: Laurent Dubois

media:content.media:credit: Lynne Sladky/Associated Press

item.title: How South Korea Uses A.I. to Check on Its Elderly
item.description: In the world’s fastest aging society, artificial intelligence is being used to make care calls to older adults who live alone and to fight dementia.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:credit: Woohae Cho for The New York Times

item.title: In Iran, Cheetah Sightings Offer Rare Spots of Hope Amid War
item.description: Conservationists say they have recorded several new adult and cub Asiatic cheetahs, a critically endangered subspecies found only in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A female Asiatic cheetah named Dalbar in the Pardisan Park in Tehran in 2017. The species is under constant threat of hunters, speeding cars and wild dogs.

item.title: Nacimientos de bebés en casa conllevan serios peligros
item.description: Los promotores del parto libre rechazan cualquier tipo de intervención médica durante el embarazo o el parto. El movimiento ha sido tendencia en las redes sociales, pero sus críticos advierten de los graves riesgos que esta práctica conlleva.
item.dc:creator: Dawn MacKeen

media:content.media:credit: Bethany Mollenkof para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Laszlo-Rath y su hijo, ahora de 4 años, en casa

item.title: Corrections: April 28, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, April 28, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Disquieting Political Routine Of Gunfire and Death Threats
item.description: Quotation of the day for Tuesday, April 28, 2026.

item.title: British Embassy Hosts Royal Couple, and a Few Hundred More, at Garden Party
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla gathered with Washington’s famous and not so famous for an off-the-record evening.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Julia Demaree Nikhinson
media:content.media:description: King Charles III and the British ambassador to the United States, Christian Turner, pointing, attended a garden party at the British Embassy in Washington on Monday.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 28, 2026
item.description: Hal Moore’s puzzle is a feast for the eyes.
item.dc:creator: Nikita Richardson

media:content.media:credit: T.J. Kirkpatrick for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: This boot was made for driving.

item.title: Megan Thee Stallion to Leave ‘Moulin Rouge!’ on Broadway Early
item.description: The rapper will perform in “Moulin Rouge!” for the final time on Friday, though the production didn’t say why she was leaving more than two weeks early.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Herrington

media:content.media:credit: Theo Wargo/Getty Images For Hot Girl Productions
media:content.media:description: Megan Thee Stallion made her Broadway debut in “Moulin Rouge! The Musical” on March 24, and had been scheduled to appear through May 17.

item.title: Ataque a la gala de corresponsales de la Casa Blanca: estos son los cargos
item.description: Las autoridades afirman que el hombre de California irrumpió el sábado en el evento en Washington con la intención de matar al presidente.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett, Zach Montague and Michael Levenson

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche, fiscal general en funciones, dijo el lunes que los investigadores estaban trabajando para determinar si el sospechoso tenía alguna conexión con activistas de izquierda.

item.title: Cast Your Vote for the Greatest Living American Songwriters
item.description: Times critics, with the help of more than 250 experts, made their list of the 30 greatest living American songwriters. Now it’s your turn to choose.

item.title: Greatest Songwriters: How The Times Created the List
item.description: First we polled hundreds of experts. Then Times critics spent weeks arguing about what constitutes greatness in American music.

item.title: See Who Music Industry Leaders Nominated as America’s Greatest Living Songwriters
item.description: We received more than 250 expert ballots. Explore some of them here.

item.title: Do You Know Who Wrote These Hit Songs? Take Our Quiz.
item.description: Test your knowledge of some of our songwriters’ biggest hits.
item.dc:creator: Renan Borelli

item.title: The Objects That Inspire America's Greatest Songwriters
item.description: The objects and instruments (and occasional creatures) that spur the writing process.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Stevie Wonder Is the Ultimate Embodiment of American Music
item.description: These are the songs you’d play to newborns, or alien visitors, to demonstrate the purest joys of our musical tradition.

item.title: Lucinda Williams’s Songs Can Turn Any Sentiment Into a Lit Fuse
item.description: Few artists sculpt songs with this level of power and emotional intensity.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Diane Warren Is a Titan of Lavish Power Ballads You Know and Love
item.description: Her widescreen, acrobatic songs have dominated radios, karaoke nights and — most likely — a big portion of your memory.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: How Young Thug Flipped the Hip-Hop Genre on Its Head
item.description: He single-handedly changed hip-hop into something futuristic and alien — right when the genre needed it most.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Why Taylor Swift Is America’s Most Carefully Studied Songwriter
item.description: By crafting vivid chronicles of her life since she was a teenager, she has built a vast catalog that rewards the attention millions have paid to it.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Bruce Springsteen Has Always Believed That Songs Can Change America
item.description: In every era, he writes like a steward of the country’s conscience — as if he has a duty to us all.

item.title: Valerie Simpson Is Behind Some of the Most Transcendent Songs You Know
item.description: Along with Nickolas Ashford, she wrote a jaw-dropping number of soaring, undeniable classics.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Paul Simon Can Compress a Novel’s Worth of Ideas Into One Song
item.description: He sets words skipping cleverly through his music. But his rhythms, melodies and harmonies are just as deft and adventurous.

item.title: Romeo Santos Laid the Groundwork for Latin Music in the 21st Century
item.description: He melded Dominican traditions with modern pop and made them a global phenomenon — changing the whole sound of modern romance.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Nile Rodgers Brings Gritty Glamor and Big Ideas to the Dance Floor
item.description: He helped make the spirit of 1970s New York a global ideal, immortalized in anthems of freedom and transgression.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Smokey Robinson Designed the Love Song as We Know It
item.description: He has written dozens of classic songs for classic acts — all in lucid, crystalline language, with a timeless elegance that still sets them soaring.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Lionel Richie's R&B Smash Songs Were the Soundtrack of an Era
item.description: He’s written heaps of our most indelibly gorgeous, time-stoppingly gentle hits — for the Commodores, for himself and for other pop legends.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Dolly Parton’s Sparkle Masks Her Rigorous Songwriting Craft
item.description: High-femme glamour, down-home simplicity, endless creativity — and such a generous, approachable spirit, it’s easy to forget how great her writing is.

item.title: How Outkast Made Hip-Hop More Eccentric, And More Universal
item.description: Their songs leaned into regional detail and unpredictable ideas. The more specific they got, the broader the audience they captivated.

item.title: How Willie Nelson Makes Intricate Songwriting Seem Effortless
item.description: His writing is wildly sophisticated, full of jazzy complexity — and yet it always feels plain-spoken and perfectly at ease.

item.title: Stephin Merritt Is the American Songbook’s Most Mischievous Student
item.description: He twists, ironizes and interrogates the tradition of songwriting, turning it into a profound and comic reflection of itself.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Lana Del Rey Injected Perverse New Moods Into American Pop
item.description: By delighting in the things women aren’t supposed to say, her songs bewitch listeners with an emotional register they never would have expected.

item.title: Babyface Explains How He Wrote Hits for Madonna, Whitney Houston and More
item.description: The craftsman behind dozens of hits for performers like Whitney Houston and Boyz II Men talks about how he wrote the love songs that defined a decade.

item.title: Kendrick Lamar Can Be Gloriously Profound, and Gloriously Petty
item.description: His virtuosity, and seriousness of purpose, made him the Dylanesque voice of hip-hop. He can also cut you when he needs to.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Mariah Explains Her Hit-Producing Songwriting Process
item.description: The artist talks about writing her era-defining hits, the pleasures of collaboration and some of the ways she has been undercredited as a writer.

item.title: Carole King's Songwriting Defined Melancholy for a Generation of Women
item.description: A legend of pop craftsmanship. A trailblazer for singer-songwriter intimacy. Either feat alone could have earned a spot on this list.

item.title: Lucinda Williams Explains What It Takes to Write a Great Song
item.description: The singer-songwriter talks about being self-taught, and reaching down into the deepest, darkest parts of herself to pull out a song.

item.title: Nile Rodgers Explains How He Wrote ‘We Are Family,’ ‘Le Freak’ and More
item.description: The co-founder of Chic, the de facto house band of New York’s late-70s disco boom, talks about taking inspiration from the city’s club scene.

item.title: Three of Nashville’s Finest Writers Explain How Country Hits Get Written
item.description: Shane McAnally, Brandy Clark and Josh Osborne talk about songwriting as a day job and the art of telling simple truths in a single line.

item.title: Jay-Z’s Lyrical Ingenuity Stretches From Street-Level Grit to Midlife Reflection
item.description: He rose to fame as a steely-eyed chronicler of his youth. Then he carved a path to become rap’s elder statesman.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times

item.title: Jimmy Jam and Terry Lewis Kept Pop on the Cutting Edge
item.description: The sonic ingenuity they brought to scores of hits — from machine-tooled drums to glittering harmonies — helped make the charts a home for surprising sounds.

media:content.media:credit: Stefan Ruiz for The New York Times
